title: Engineering Mathematics - Discrete Mathematics - UGC NET CSE  June 2012  Part 3  Question

sections.name: UGC NET CSE  June 2012  Part 3  Question

questions.question:
Let \( Q(x,y) \) denote “x+y=0” and let there be two quantifications given as

  1. \( \exists y \forall x Q(x,y) \)
  2. \( \forall x \exists y Q(x,y) \)

where \( x \) and \( y \) are real numbers. Then which of the following is valid?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 17
options: I is true and II is false
I is false and II is true
I is false and II is also false
questions.correct_answer: I is false and II is true

questions.question:
How many relations are there on a set with n elements that are symmetric and a set with n elements that are reflexive and symmetric?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 50

Consider a set \( A = \left\{1, 2, 3, …….., 1000\right\} \).

How many members of A shall be divisible by \( 3 \) or by \( 5 \) or by both \( 3 \) and \( 5 \)?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 2 | Question: 01

The objective of ____ procedure is to discover at least one ____ that causes two literals to match.



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 24

Which one of the following is true?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 24
options: The resolvent of two Horn clauses is not a Horn clause
The resolvent of two Horn clauses is a Horn clause
If we resolve a negated goal G against a fact or rule A to get clause C then C has positive literal or non-null goal
If we resolve a negated goal G against a fact or rule A to get clause C then C has positive literal or null goal
questions.correct_answer: The resolvent of two Horn clauses is a Horn clause

questions.question:
If \( A_i = \{-i, \dots , -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, \dots , i \} \) then \( \cup_{i=1}^\infty A_i \) is



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | July 2018 | Part 2 | Question: 86

Let \( G \) be a simple undirected graph, \( T_D \) be a DFS tree on \( G \), and \( T_B \) be the BFS tree on \( G \). Consider the following statements.

Statement \( I \): No edge of \( G \) is a cross with respect to \( T_D \)

Statement \( II \): For every edge \( (u,v) \) of \( G \), if \( u \) is at depth \( i \) and \( v \) is at depth \( j \) in \( T_B \) then \( \mid i-j \mid =1 \)

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 86
options: Both Statement \( I \) and Statement \( II \) are true
Both Statement \( I \) and Statement \( II \) are false
Statement \( I \) is correct but Statement \( II \) is false
Statement \( I \) is incorrect but Statement \( II \) is true
questions.correct_answer: Statement \( I \) is correct but Statement \( II \) is false

questions.question:
What kind of clauses are available in conjunctive normal form?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 38

If \( f(x)=x \) is my friend, and \( p(x) =x \) is perfect, then correct logical translation of the statement “some of my friends are not perfect” is ______



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 37

Let \( G \) be a directed graph whose vertex set is the set of numbers from \( 1 \) to \( 100 \). There is an edge from a vertex \( i \) to a vertex \( j \) if and only if either \( j=i+1 \) or \( j=3i \). The minimum number of edges in a path in \( G \) from vertex \( 1 \) to vertex \( 100 \) is ______



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 26

Which of the following pairs of propositions are not logically equivalent?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 3
options: \( ((p \wedge q) \vee (\neg p \wedge \neg q)) \) and \( p \leftrightarrow q \)
